summary: On his 33rd birthday, Hotz reflects on consciousness, technological progress, and the inevitability of superhuman AI. He argues that consciousness is merely an illusion created by neural networks, and that technology inexorably moves challenges from difficult to trivial. Drawing on the Unabomber manifesto, he categorizes human drives by difficulty level and predicts AI will eventually relearn and surpass all of human culture, just as AlphaZero did for chess. The post ends as a recruitment pitch for comma.ai, positioning it among the major AI labs pursuing different paths to artificial general intelligence.
key_points: Consciousness is an illusion - we are 'randomly initialized dot products' tricked into believing in selfhood
Technology continuously moves type 2 challenges (requiring serious effort) into type 1 (minimal effort), eliminating meaningful human struggle
Culture is downstream of technology, and technology only moves forward - progress toward superhuman AI is inevitable
AlphaZero didn't just beat humans at chess, it relearned all of chess culture from scratch - AI will do the same for human culture
Self-driving is essentially solved; the next challenge is to 'solve life' itself
money_quotes: You are a bunch of randomly initialized dot products with some quasiconvex optimizer that's tricked you into believing you are you.
Consciousness is an atheistic synonym for soul.
Culture is downstream of technology. And it is technology that only swims forward.
As it was for chess, 'human level' is a meaningless point. The world doesn't care how you feel about this fact.
It didn't just learn to play chess, it relearned all of chess culture.
Self driving is solved sans a bunch of bugfixes and scaling. Now let's solve life.
Type 2 things will require great effort to be kept around.
key_insight: Superhuman AI is inevitable and will not merely match human capabilities but completely relearn and transcend human culture itself, rendering 'human level' a meaningless waypoint.
